ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tweet was created */,
  "id_str" TEXT /* ID of the tweet in string format */,
  "full_text" TEXT /* Full text of the tweet */,
  "truncated" INTEGER /* Whether the tweet text is truncated */,
  "display_text_range" TEXT /* Range of characters in the tweet text */,
  "entities" TEXT /* Entities mentioned in the tweet (e.g., hashtags, user mentions) */,
  "extended_entities" TEXT /* Extended entities mentioned in the tweet (e.g., media, URLs) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter Web Client) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "favorite_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been favorited */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "conversation_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the conversation thread */,
  "in_reply_to_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being replied to */,
  "in_reply_to_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being replied to */,
  "quoted_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being quoted */,
  "quoted_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being quoted */,
  "lang" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "place" TEXT /* Geographical place of the tweet */,
  "coordinates" TEXT /* Geographical coordinates of the tweet */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the tweet to track changes over time */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "screen_name" TEXT /* Username (handle) of the user */,
  "description" TEXT /* Bio or description of the user */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "friends_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user follows */,
  "favourites_count" INTEGER /* Number of tweets the user has favorited */,
  "listed_count" INTEGER /* Number of lists the user is a member of */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the user is verified */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, data scientist, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "entities" (
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each entity */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that mentions the entity */,
  "entity_type" TEXT /* Type of entity (e.g., hashtag, user mention) */,
  "entity_text" TEXT /* Text of the entity */,
  "start_index" INTEGER /* Start index of the entity in the tweet text */,
  "end_index" INTEGER /* End index of the entity in the tweet text */,
  "entity_url" TEXT /* URL associated with the entity (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_entities_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "extended_entities" (
  "extended_entity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each extended entity */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that mentions the extended entity */,
  "extended_entity_type" TEXT /* Type of extended entity (e.g., media, URL) */,
  "extended_entity_text" TEXT /* Text of the extended entity */,
  "start_index" INTEGER /* Start index of the extended entity in the tweet text */,
  "end_index" INTEGER /* End index of the extended entity in the tweet text */,
  "entity_url" TEXT /* URL associated with the extended entity (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("extended_entity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_extended_entities_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., web, mobile, API) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "in_reply_to_statuses" (
  "in_reply_to_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being replied to */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that is a reply */,
  "in_reply_to_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being replied to */,
  "in_reply_to_screen_name" TEXT /* Username (handle) of the user being replied to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("in_reply_to_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_in_reply_to_statuses_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contributors" (
  "contributor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contributor */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that the contributor contributed to */,
  "contributor_name" TEXT /* Name of the contributor */,
  "contributor_screen_name" TEXT /* Username (handle) of the contributor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contributor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contributors_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quote_statuses" (
  "quote_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being quoted */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that quotes the original tweet */,
  "quoted_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being quoted */,
  "quoted_tweet_text" TEXT /* Text of the quoted tweet */,
  "quoted_tweet_created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the quoted tweet was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quote_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quote_statuses_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retweets" (
  "retweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the retweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet */,
  "retweeted_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who retweeted the original tweet */,
  "retweeted_tweet_text" TEXT /* Text of the retweeted tweet */,
  "retweeted_tweet_created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the retweeted tweet was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "favorites" (
  "favorite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the favorite */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was favorited */,
  "favorited_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who favorited the tweet */,
  "favorited_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tweet was favorited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("favorite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_favorites_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensitive_info" (
  "sensitive_info_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensitive info record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that the sensitive info record belongs to */,
  "possibly_sensitive" INTEGER /* Whether the tweet is possibly sensitive */,
  "possibly_sensitive_appealable" INTEGER /* Whether the tweet's sensitivity is appealable */,
  "sensitive_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the tweet being marked as sensitive (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensitive_info_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensitive_info_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that the language record belongs to */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_languages_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
